In an interview, artist Blake "Keo" Lethem states that the original mask for DOOM was made of plastic with red stripes and two breathing holes. You can see it at 2:19 in the ? music video. It was no longer used due to it being weak and fragile, as it was made of plastic.

A newspaper advertisment for Operation: Doomsday was planned. The advertisement would've been an edited comic book panel of DOOM as Doctor Doom in a helicopter exclaming, "Now I unleash my evil plot!", however before it was going to be pressed DOOM wanted to change it from "evil" to "live". Due to them not being able to make the change in time, the advertisement was scrapped.

Name (Check Out The Website Tracker!): MF DOOM X MF GRIMM Album [V1]
Notes: In a 2002 interview, MF GRIMM said he was interested in a collaborative album with MF DOOM, however he stated he wanted Canto II (Most likely Digital Tears: E-Mail From Purgatory) to be his last album. MF Grimm later recorded more albums after Digital Tears, but it's unknown if this concept for the album was going to ever be recorded. Was likely shelved when DOOM dissed him on El Chupa Nibre. Not to be confused with Special Blends.

DOOM and Ye apparently got together in the studio for a single night once, the voicemail from the Gazillion Ear remix is from that linkup. Reliable sources have said they didn’t really record anything together that night though, mostly just talked and played a few beats. Kanye did come up with the idea of doing a collab and calling it either “MADVILLAINZ” or “SUPERVILLAINZ”  where he played a character called Khan, but nothing came of it.
According to multiple sources, Nas had wanted to make a song with DOOM, however nothing seems to have come out of it.

Tight Bros. was a proposed show by MC Chris and MF Doom. Originally scheduled to air in Febuary 2010, chris confirmed the show is not happening during a Reddit AMA, he claims several episodes were made. the theme song Doom Made Was Released By someone who worked on the show after dooms death

While not actually a documentary, a live showing of the MM.. FOOD Drive Tour sponsored by Spotify was held at Nitehawk Cinema in Brooklyn, New York. It supposedly featured food such as the "Deep Fried Frenz" chicken tender plate with "Vomitspit" buttermilk ranch dip, a "Fig Leaf Bi-Carbonate" cocktail, and two "Poo Putt Platter" dishes that were meat and cheese charcuterie boards. Most likely features differences from the original DVD, as you can see altered MM.. FOOD 20th anniversary artwork on the screen.
